- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

1	# WP-041 — TOP-V3-01: Toposolid primitive
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-top-v3-01-toposolid`
4	**Effort:** L — 2.5 weeks
5	**Status:** `now` — gating primitive for T7; no other WP in the theme is reachable without this.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T7 TOP-V3-01 entry (line 2557); §3 Design Pillars + anti-patterns A1–A10 (line 73); §A Agent Instructions (line 3842)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/document.py` — existing element kinds, `Document.elements` union
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `Command` union; add toposolid commands here
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — command dispatch; add handlers here
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py` — IFC export pipeline; add `IfcGeographicElement` path here
19	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/site/sun_position.py` — existing site module structure (model `app/bim_ai/site/toposolid.py` after this)
20	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — all element types; add `Toposolid` here
21	
22	---
23	
24	## Verbatim TOP-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T7, line 2557)
25	
26	#### TOP-V3-01 — Toposolid primitive
27	
28	**Status.** `now` — gating primitive for T7. Without this, no other WP in the theme is reachable.
29	
30	**Scope.** Terrain as a kernel-first solid (not a mesh, not a surface). Closed XY footprint polygon with a heightmap (or per-vertex z values) lifting the surface, plus uniform thickness drawing the underside. Hostable (floors sit on it), subtractable (you can excavate a basement), renderable in plan + section + 3D.
31	
32	**Data model.**
33	
34	```ts
35	{
36	  kind: 'toposolid';
37	  id: string;
38	  name?: string;
39	  boundaryMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }[];   // closed polygon
40	  heightSamples: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number }[];   // sparse points
41	  // OR: heightmapGridMm?: { stepMm: number; rows: number; cols: number; values: number[] };
42	  thicknessMm: number;                          // default 1500
43	  baseElevationMm?: number;                     // datum for the underside
44	  defaultMaterialKey?: string;                  // overridden per subdivision (TOP-V3-02)
45	  pinned?: boolean;
46	}
47	```
48	
49	Two parametrisations: sparse height samples (surveyor data) or regular grid (DEM raster). Engine triangulates either into a Delaunay surface for rendering and CSG.
50	
51	**Engine.** New Python element kind; new `createToposolid` / `updateToposolid` / `deleteToposolid` commands. Engine triangulates samples, lifts to a solid by extruding downward by `thicknessMm`. CSG-aware so basements and footings can subtract from it. New advisor rule `toposolid_pierce_check` fires when a foundation overlaps without a recorded `slab_opening` or excavation.
52	
53	**UI.** Architecture ribbon → Site → "Toposolid". Two paths: (a) draw closed boundary in plan + uniform z (flat starter), (b) import heightmap from DEM data fetched alongside OSM-V3-01. Plan canvas renders contour lines at 0.5 m intervals; 3D renders the solid; section shows the underside.
54	
55	**Acceptance.** `bim-ai create-toposolid --boundary "..." --thickness-mm 1500 --height-samples "..."` produces a renderable solid. A floor placed on it inherits surface elevation. CSG with a building footprint produces a clean cut. IFC export round-trips as `IfcGeographicElement`.
56	
57	**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks. Risk: CSG against non-trivial footprints; v2 wall-recess + dormer CSG is the precedent.
58	
59	**Cross-theme references.** KRN-06 (shared coords carryover — `baseElevationMm` is expressed in project shared-coordinates). Gates TOP-V3-02, TOP-V3-03, TOP-V3-04, OSM-V3-01, LOT-V3-02.
60	
61	**Source.** R-D §3 G (site/terrain gap); T7 theme keystone decision; user reframe 2026-05-08.
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Setup
66	
67	```bash
68	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
69	git checkout -b feat/v3-top-v3-01-toposolid
70	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
71	python -c "from bim_ai.document import Document; print('document ok')"
72	uv sync && pnpm install
73	```
74	
75	---
76	
77	## Implementation tasks
78	
79	### 1. Python element model (`app/bim_ai/document.py`)
80	
81	Add two new element models and register them in `Document.elements`:
82	
83	```python
84	class HeightSample(BaseModel):
85	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
86	    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
87	    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
88	    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")
89	
90	class HeightmapGrid(BaseModel):
91	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
92	    step_mm: float = Field(alias="stepMm")
93	    rows: int
94	    cols: int
95	    values: list[float]   # row-major, len == rows * cols
96	
97	class BoundaryPoint(BaseModel):
98	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
99	    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
100	    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
101	
102	class Toposolid(BaseModel):
103	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
104	    kind: Literal["toposolid"] = "toposolid"
105	    id: str
106	    name: str | None = None
107	    boundary_mm: list[BoundaryPoint] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
108	    height_samples: list[HeightSample] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="heightSamples")
109	    heightmap_grid_mm: HeightmapGrid | None = Field(default=None, alias="heightmapGridMm")
110	    thickness_mm: float = Field(default=1500.0, alias="thicknessMm")
111	    base_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseElevationMm")
112	    default_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultMaterialKey")
113	    pinned: bool = False
114	```
115	
116	Add `Toposolid` to the `Document.elements` union discriminated on `kind`.
117	
118	### 2. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)
119	
120	Add to the `Command` union:
121	
122	```python
123	class CreateToposolidCmd(BaseModel):
124	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
125	    type: Literal["CreateToposolid"] = "CreateToposolid"
126	    toposolid_id: str = Field(alias="toposolidId")
127	    name: str | None = None
128	    boundary_mm: list[dict] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
129	    height_samples: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="heightSamples")
130	    heightmap_grid_mm: dict | None = Field(default=None, alias="heightmapGridMm")
131	    thickness_mm: float = Field(default=1500.0, alias="thicknessMm")
132	    base_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseElevationMm")
133	    default_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultMaterialKey")
134	
135	class UpdateToposolidCmd(BaseModel):
136	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
137	    type: Literal["UpdateToposolid"] = "UpdateToposolid"
138	    toposolid_id: str = Field(alias="toposolidId")
139	    name: str | None = None
140	    thickness_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="thicknessMm")
141	    base_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseElevationMm")
142	    default_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultMaterialKey")
143	    pinned: bool | None = None
144	
145	class DeleteToposolidCmd(BaseModel):
146	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
147	    type: Literal["DeleteToposolid"] = "DeleteToposolid"
148	    toposolid_id: str = Field(alias="toposolidId")
149	```
150	
151	### 3. Engine handlers (`app/bim_ai/engine.py`)
152	
153	**`CreateToposolidCmd`** — construct a `Toposolid` from the command fields; validate that `boundary_mm` has at least 3 points and forms a closed polygon (first ≠ last is fine — engine closes it); validate that exactly one of `height_samples` or `heightmap_grid_mm` is provided (or `height_samples` is empty for a flat starter). Store in `doc.elements`.
154	
155	**`UpdateToposolidCmd`** — patch only the provided (non-None) fields on the existing `Toposolid`. Raise if the element does not exist or has `kind != 'toposolid'`.
156	
157	**`DeleteToposolidCmd`** — remove the element. Fire a warning (not an error) if any floor element references this toposolid as its host.
158	
159	**Advisory rule — `toposolid_pierce_check`** — after `CreateToposolidCmd` and `UpdateToposolidCmd`, walk all slab/foundation elements in the document; if any element's footprint XY overlaps the toposolid boundary and no `slab_opening` or excavation is recorded, emit an advisory of severity `warning`.
160	
161	### 4. Triangulation helper (`app/bim_ai/site/toposolid.py`)
162	
163	New file implementing the Delaunay triangulation surface:
164	
165	```python
166	"""Toposolid geometry helpers — Delaunay triangulation + contour extraction."""
167	from __future__ import annotations
168	from bim_ai.document import Toposolid, HeightSample
169	
170	def samples_from_toposolid(topo: Toposolid) -> list[tuple[float, float, float]]:
171	    """Return (xMm, yMm, zMm) tuples from whichever parametrisation is active."""
172	    if topo.heightmap_grid_mm:
173	        g = topo.heightmap_grid_mm
174	        pts = []
175	        for r in range(g.rows):
176	            for c in range(g.cols):
177	                x = c * g.step_mm
178	                y = r * g.step_mm
179	                z = g.values[r * g.cols + c]
180	                pts.append((x, y, z))
181	        return pts
182	    return [(s.x_mm, s.y_mm, s.z_mm) for s in topo.height_samples]
183	
184	def contour_polylines(topo: Toposolid, interval_mm: float = 500.0) -> list[list[tuple[float, float]]]:
185	    """Return a list of XY polylines representing contour lines at `interval_mm` spacing.
186	
187	    Uses linear interpolation between triangulated edges. Returns empty list when
188	    fewer than 3 samples are present (flat-starter case).
189	    """
190	    pts = samples_from_toposolid(topo)
191	    if len(pts) < 3:
192	        return []
193	    # Minimal marching-squares / edge-interpolation implementation.
194	    # Real implementation uses scipy.spatial.Delaunay when available,
195	    # falls back to a simple planar interpolation for the flat-starter case.
196	    z_vals = [p[2] for p in pts]
197	    z_min = min(z_vals)
198	    z_max = max(z_vals)
199	    levels = []
200	    z = z_min + interval_mm
201	    while z < z_max:
202	        levels.append(z)
203	        z += interval_mm
204	    # Return one dummy polyline per level as a stub — renderer fills in real geometry.
205	    return [[] for _ in levels]
206	
207	def underside_elevation_mm(topo: Toposolid) -> float:
208	    """Elevation of the flat underside of the toposolid solid."""
209	    base = topo.base_elevation_mm or 0.0
210	    return base - topo.thickness_mm
211	```
212	
213	### 5. IFC export (`app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py`)
214	
215	Add a handler for `Toposolid` elements that exports each instance as an `IfcGeographicElement` with `PredefinedType = TERRAIN`. The geometric representation is a triangulated face set built from `samples_from_toposolid`. If `ifcopenshell` is unavailable, skip silently (same guard as the existing export).
216	
217	### 6. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)
218	
219	```ts
220	export type BoundaryPoint = { xMm: number; yMm: number };
221	export type HeightSample = { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number };
222	export type HeightmapGrid = {
223	  stepMm: number;
224	  rows: number;
225	  cols: number;
226	  values: number[];
227	};
228	
229	export type Toposolid = {
230	  kind: 'toposolid';
231	  id: string;
232	  name?: string;
233	  boundaryMm: BoundaryPoint[];
234	  heightSamples?: HeightSample[];
235	  heightmapGridMm?: HeightmapGrid;
236	  thicknessMm: number;
237	  baseElevationMm?: number;
238	  defaultMaterialKey?: string;
239	  pinned?: boolean;
240	};
241	```
242	
243	Add `Toposolid` to the `Element` union type.
244	
245	### 7. Tests (`app/tests/test_top_v3_01.py`)
246	
247	Cover:
248	
249	- `CreateToposolidCmd` with sparse height samples creates a `Toposolid` element.
250	- `CreateToposolidCmd` with a `heightmapGridMm` grid creates a `Toposolid` element.
251	- Flat starter (empty `heightSamples`, no grid) is accepted — models a perfectly flat terrain.
252	- `UpdateToposolidCmd` patches `thicknessMm` without touching other fields.
253	- `UpdateToposolidCmd` on a missing ID raises.
254	- `DeleteToposolidCmd` removes the element.
255	- `samples_from_toposolid` returns correct tuples for both parametrisations.
256	- `contour_polylines` returns an empty list for a flat-starter (fewer than 3 samples).
257	- `underside_elevation_mm` returns `baseElevationMm - thicknessMm`.
258	- Advisory `toposolid_pierce_check` fires when a slab footprint overlaps the toposolid boundary.
259	- Round-trip: `Document` serialises and deserialises with a `Toposolid` element intact.
260	
261	---
262	
263	## Implementation rules
264	
265	These are verbatim from §A Agent Instructions:
266	
267	- **Honour API-V3-01.** Every new kernel command (`CreateToposolidCmd`, `UpdateToposolidCmd`, `DeleteToposolidCmd`) must have CLI entry in `packages/cli/cli.mjs` + Pydantic schema in `commands.py` + JSON schema. Add tests at `tests/cli/` or `tests/api/` if the CLI layer exists for other commands; if not, note the gap in the final report.
268	- **No scope creep.** This WP is the primitive only. Subdivisions (TOP-V3-02), paint UX (TOP-V3-03), site walls (TOP-V3-04), and OSM import (OSM-V3-01) are separate WPs. Do not implement them here, even partially.
269	- **Design-pillar adherence.** UI paths must follow D1 (direct manipulation first) and D8 (context, not configuration). The plan-canvas contour rendering must use `--draft-lw-*` tokens and `--draft-paper` background. Do not introduce hard-coded line widths.
270	- **No hex literals in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.** Use CSS token variables only. The ESLint rule will fail the build if you do.
271	- **Additive schema.** Existing documents with no `toposolid` elements round-trip unchanged. `Toposolid` is a new discriminated variant only.
272	- **Advisory, not error.** `toposolid_pierce_check` emits a warning advisory — it does not block the command from committing.
273	
274	---
275	
276	## Verify gate
277	
278	```bash
279	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
280	make verify
281	pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
282	cd app
283	python -m pytest tests/test_top_v3_01.py -v
284	python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
285	```
286	
287	All steps must be green before pushing. `make verify` covers format-check, lint, architecture, typecheck, pytest, and vite build.
288	
289	---
290	
291	## Commit and push
292	
293	```bash
294	git add app/bim_ai/document.py \
295	        app/bim_ai/commands.py \
296	        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
297	        app/bim_ai/site/toposolid.py \
298	        app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py \
299	        app/tests/test_top_v3_01.py \
300	        packages/core/src/index.ts
301	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
302	feat(top): TOP-V3-01 toposolid primitive — terrain solid + heightmap + thickness
303	
304	Toposolid element kind (sparse samples + grid parametrisation); CreateToposolid /
305	UpdateToposolid / DeleteToposolid commands + engine handlers; Delaunay triangulation
306	helper + contour_polylines + underside_elevation_mm; toposolid_pierce_check advisory;
307	IfcGeographicElement export; TypeScript Toposolid type.
308	
309	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
310	EOF
311	)"
312	git push -u origin feat/v3-top-v3-01-toposolid
313	```
314	
315	Do NOT open a PR. Do NOT merge to main.
316	
317	---
318

# WP-041 — TOP-V3-01: Toposolid primitive

**Branch:** `feat/v3-top-v3-01-toposolid`
**Effort:** L — 2.5 weeks
**Status:** `now` — gating primitive for T7; no other WP in the theme is reachable without this.

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T7 TOP-V3-01 entry (line 2557); §3 Design Pillars + anti-patterns A1–A10 (line 73); §A Agent Instructions (line 3842)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/document.py` — existing element kinds, `Document.elements` union
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `Command` union; add toposolid commands here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — command dispatch; add handlers here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py` — IFC export pipeline; add `IfcGeographicElement` path here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/site/sun_position.py` — existing site module structure (model `app/bim_ai/site/toposolid.py` after this)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — all element types; add `Toposolid` here

---

## Verbatim TOP-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T7, line 2557)

#### TOP-V3-01 — Toposolid primitive

**Status.** `now` — gating primitive for T7. Without this, no other WP in the theme is reachable.

**Scope.** Terrain as a kernel-first solid (not a mesh, not a surface). Closed XY footprint polygon with a heightmap (or per-vertex z values) lifting the surface, plus uniform thickness drawing the underside. Hostable (floors sit on it), subtractable (you can excavate a basement), renderable in plan + section + 3D.

**Data model.**

```ts
{
  kind: 'toposolid';
  id: string;
  name?: string;
  boundaryMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }[];   // closed polygon
  heightSamples: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number }[];   // sparse points
  // OR: heightmapGridMm?: { stepMm: number; rows: number; cols: number; values: number[] };
  thicknessMm: number;                          // default 1500
  baseElevationMm?: number;                     // datum for the underside
  defaultMaterialKey?: string;                  // overridden per subdivision (TOP-V3-02)
  pinned?: boolean;
}
```

Two parametrisations: sparse height samples (surveyor data) or regular grid (DEM raster). Engine triangulates either into a Delaunay surface for rendering and CSG.

**Engine.** New Python element kind; new `createToposolid` / `updateToposolid` / `deleteToposolid` commands. Engine triangulates samples, lifts to a solid by extruding downward by `thicknessMm`. CSG-aware so basements and footings can subtract from it. New advisor rule `toposolid_pierce_check` fires when a foundation overlaps without a recorded `slab_opening` or excavation.

**UI.** Architecture ribbon → Site → "Toposolid". Two paths: (a) draw closed boundary in plan + uniform z (flat starter), (b) import heightmap from DEM data fetched alongside OSM-V3-01. Plan canvas renders contour lines at 0.5 m intervals; 3D renders the solid; section shows the underside.

**Acceptance.** `bim-ai create-toposolid --boundary "..." --thickness-mm 1500 --height-samples "..."` produces a renderable solid. A floor placed on it inherits surface elevation. CSG with a building footprint produces a clean cut. IFC export round-trips as `IfcGeographicElement`.

**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks. Risk: CSG against non-trivial footprints; v2 wall-recess + dormer CSG is the precedent.

**Cross-theme references.** KRN-06 (shared coords carryover — `baseElevationMm` is expressed in project shared-coordinates). Gates TOP-V3-02, TOP-V3-03, TOP-V3-04, OSM-V3-01, LOT-V3-02.

**Source.** R-D §3 G (site/terrain gap); T7 theme keystone decision; user reframe 2026-05-08.

---

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-top-v3-01-toposolid
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
python -c "from bim_ai.document import Document; print('document ok')"
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Python element model (`app/bim_ai/document.py`)

Add two new element models and register them in `Document.elements`:

```python
class HeightSample(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")

class HeightmapGrid(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    step_mm: float = Field(alias="stepMm")
    rows: int
    cols: int
    values: list[float]   # row-major, len == rows * cols

class BoundaryPoint(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")

class Toposolid(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["toposolid"] = "toposolid"
    id: str
    name: str | None = None
    boundary_mm: list[BoundaryPoint] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    height_samples: list[HeightSample] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="heightSamples")
    heightmap_grid_mm: HeightmapGrid | None = Field(default=None, alias="heightmapGridMm")
    thickness_mm: float = Field(default=1500.0, alias="thicknessMm")
    base_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseElevationMm")
    default_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultMaterialKey")
    pinned: bool = False
```

Add `Toposolid` to the `Document.elements` union discriminated on `kind`.

### 2. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

Add to the `Command` union:

```python
class CreateToposolidCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["CreateToposolid"] = "CreateToposolid"
    toposolid_id: str = Field(alias="toposolidId")
    name: str | None = None
    boundary_mm: list[dict] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    height_samples: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="heightSamples")
    heightmap_grid_mm: dict | None = Field(default=None, alias="heightmapGridMm")
    thickness_mm: float = Field(default=1500.0, alias="thicknessMm")
    base_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseElevationMm")
    default_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultMaterialKey")

class UpdateToposolidCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["UpdateToposolid"] = "UpdateToposolid"
    toposolid_id: str = Field(alias="toposolidId")
    name: str | None = None
    thickness_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="thicknessMm")
    base_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseElevationMm")
    default_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultMaterialKey")
    pinned: bool | None = None

class DeleteToposolidCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["DeleteToposolid"] = "DeleteToposolid"
    toposolid_id: str = Field(alias="toposolidId")
```

### 3. Engine handlers (`app/bim_ai/engine.py`)

**`CreateToposolidCmd`** — construct a `Toposolid` from the command fields; validate that `boundary_mm` has at least 3 points and forms a closed polygon (first ≠ last is fine — engine closes it); validate that exactly one of `height_samples` or `heightmap_grid_mm` is provided (or `height_samples` is empty for a flat starter). Store in `doc.elements`.

**`UpdateToposolidCmd`** — patch only the provided (non-None) fields on the existing `Toposolid`. Raise if the element does not exist or has `kind != 'toposolid'`.

**`DeleteToposolidCmd`** — remove the element. Fire a warning (not an error) if any floor element references this toposolid as its host.

**Advisory rule — `toposolid_pierce_check`** — after `CreateToposolidCmd` and `UpdateToposolidCmd`, walk all slab/foundation elements in the document; if any element's footprint XY overlaps the toposolid boundary and no `slab_opening` or excavation is recorded, emit an advisory of severity `warning`.

### 4. Triangulation helper (`app/bim_ai/site/toposolid.py`)

New file implementing the Delaunay triangulation surface:

```python
"""Toposolid geometry helpers — Delaunay triangulation + contour extraction."""
from __future__ import annotations
from bim_ai.document import Toposolid, HeightSample

def samples_from_toposolid(topo: Toposolid) -> list[tuple[float, float, float]]:
    """Return (xMm, yMm, zMm) tuples from whichever parametrisation is active."""
    if topo.heightmap_grid_mm:
        g = topo.heightmap_grid_mm
        pts = []
        for r in range(g.rows):
            for c in range(g.cols):
                x = c * g.step_mm
                y = r * g.step_mm
                z = g.values[r * g.cols + c]
                pts.append((x, y, z))
        return pts
    return [(s.x_mm, s.y_mm, s.z_mm) for s in topo.height_samples]

def contour_polylines(topo: Toposolid, interval_mm: float = 500.0) -> list[list[tuple[float, float]]]:
    """Return a list of XY polylines representing contour lines at `interval_mm` spacing.

    Uses linear interpolation between triangulated edges. Returns empty list when
    fewer than 3 samples are present (flat-starter case).
    """
    pts = samples_from_toposolid(topo)
    if len(pts) < 3:
        return []
    # Minimal marching-squares / edge-interpolation implementation.
    # Real implementation uses scipy.spatial.Delaunay when available,
    # falls back to a simple planar interpolation for the flat-starter case.
    z_vals = [p[2] for p in pts]
    z_min = min(z_vals)
    z_max = max(z_vals)
    levels = []
    z = z_min + interval_mm
    while z < z_max:
        levels.append(z)
        z += interval_mm
    # Return one dummy polyline per level as a stub — renderer fills in real geometry.
    return [[] for _ in levels]

def underside_elevation_mm(topo: Toposolid) -> float:
    """Elevation of the flat underside of the toposolid solid."""
    base = topo.base_elevation_mm or 0.0
    return base - topo.thickness_mm
```

### 5. IFC export (`app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py`)

Add a handler for `Toposolid` elements that exports each instance as an `IfcGeographicElement` with `PredefinedType = TERRAIN`. The geometric representation is a triangulated face set built from `samples_from_toposolid`. If `ifcopenshell` is unavailable, skip silently (same guard as the existing export).

### 6. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type BoundaryPoint = { xMm: number; yMm: number };
export type HeightSample = { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number };
export type HeightmapGrid = {
  stepMm: number;
  rows: number;
  cols: number;
  values: number[];
};

export type Toposolid = {
  kind: 'toposolid';
  id: string;
  name?: string;
  boundaryMm: BoundaryPoint[];
  heightSamples?: HeightSample[];
  heightmapGridMm?: HeightmapGrid;
  thicknessMm: number;
  baseElevationMm?: number;
  defaultMaterialKey?: string;
  pinned?: boolean;
};
```

Add `Toposolid` to the `Element` union type.

### 7. Tests (`app/tests/test_top_v3_01.py`)

Cover:

- `CreateToposolidCmd` with sparse height samples creates a `Toposolid` element.
- `CreateToposolidCmd` with a `heightmapGridMm` grid creates a `Toposolid` element.
- Flat starter (empty `heightSamples`, no grid) is accepted — models a perfectly flat terrain.
- `UpdateToposolidCmd` patches `thicknessMm` without touching other fields.
- `UpdateToposolidCmd` on a missing ID raises.
- `DeleteToposolidCmd` removes the element.
- `samples_from_toposolid` returns correct tuples for both parametrisations.
- `contour_polylines` returns an empty list for a flat-starter (fewer than 3 samples).
- `underside_elevation_mm` returns `baseElevationMm - thicknessMm`.
- Advisory `toposolid_pierce_check` fires when a slab footprint overlaps the toposolid boundary.
- Round-trip: `Document` serialises and deserialises with a `Toposolid` element intact.

---

## Implementation rules

These are verbatim from §A Agent Instructions:

- **Honour API-V3-01.** Every new kernel command (`CreateToposolidCmd`, `UpdateToposolidCmd`, `DeleteToposolidCmd`) must have CLI entry in `packages/cli/cli.mjs` + Pydantic schema in `commands.py` + JSON schema. Add tests at `tests/cli/` or `tests/api/` if the CLI layer exists for other commands; if not, note the gap in the final report.
- **No scope creep.** This WP is the primitive only. Subdivisions (TOP-V3-02), paint UX (TOP-V3-03), site walls (TOP-V3-04), and OSM import (OSM-V3-01) are separate WPs. Do not implement them here, even partially.
- **Design-pillar adherence.** UI paths must follow D1 (direct manipulation first) and D8 (context, not configuration). The plan-canvas contour rendering must use `--draft-lw-*` tokens and `--draft-paper` background. Do not introduce hard-coded line widths.
- **No hex literals in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.** Use CSS token variables only. The ESLint rule will fail the build if you do.
- **Additive schema.** Existing documents with no `toposolid` elements round-trip unchanged. `Toposolid` is a new discriminated variant only.
- **Advisory, not error.** `toposolid_pierce_check` emits a warning advisory — it does not block the command from committing.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
make verify
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
cd app
python -m pytest tests/test_top_v3_01.py -v
python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
```

All steps must be green before pushing. `make verify` covers format-check, lint, architecture, typecheck, pytest, and vite build.

---

## Commit and push

```bash
git add app/bim_ai/document.py \
        app/bim_ai/commands.py \
        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
        app/bim_ai/site/toposolid.py \
        app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py \
        app/tests/test_top_v3_01.py \
        packages/core/src/index.ts
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(top): TOP-V3-01 toposolid primitive — terrain solid + heightmap + thickness

Toposolid element kind (sparse samples + grid parametrisation); CreateToposolid /
UpdateToposolid / DeleteToposolid commands + engine handlers; Delaunay triangulation
helper + contour_polylines + underside_elevation_mm; toposolid_pierce_check advisory;
IfcGeographicElement export; TypeScript Toposolid type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-top-v3-01-toposolid
```

Do NOT open a PR. Do NOT merge to main.

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

from bim_ai.elements import Element


class DesignOptionProvenance(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    submitter: Literal["agent", "human", "ci"] = "human"
    bundle_id: str = Field(alias="bundleId")
    created_at: int = Field(alias="createdAt")


class DesignOption(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    id: str
    name: str
    is_primary: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isPrimary")
    provenance: DesignOptionProvenance | None = Field(default=None)


class DesignOptionSet(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    id: str
    name: str
    options: list[DesignOption] = Field(default_factory=list)


class Document(BaseModel):
    revision: int = 1
    elements: dict[str, Annotated[Element, Field(discriminator="kind")]]
    design_option_sets: list[DesignOptionSet] = Field(
        default_factory=list, alias="designOptionSets"
    )


from bim_ai.cmd.types import AgentTrace  # noqa: E402, F401 — needed for model_rebuild

Document.model_rebuild()

from __future__ import annotations

import math
from collections import Counter
from typing import Any

import numpy as np

from bim_ai.commands import (
    CreateFloorCmd,
    CreateLevelCmd,
    CreateRoofCmd,
    CreateRoofOpeningCmd,
    CreateRoomOutlineCmd,
    CreateSlabOpeningCmd,
    CreateStairCmd,
    CreateWallCmd,
)
from bim_ai.document import Document
from bim_ai.elements import (
    BeamElem,
    CeilingElem,
    ColumnElem,
    DoorElem,
    FloorElem,
    LevelElem,
    RailingElem,
    RoofElem,

    Vec2Mm,
    WallElem,
    WindowElem,
)
from bim_ai.ifc_material_layer_exchange_v0 import (
    kernel_ifc_material_layer_set_readback_v0,
    try_attach_kernel_ifc_material_layer_set,
    try_attach_kernel_ifc_single_material,
)
from bim_ai.ifc_property_set_coverage_evidence_v0 import (
    build_ifc_property_set_coverage_expansion_v1,
    build_kernel_ifc_property_set_coverage_evidence_v0,
    unavailable_property_set_coverage_evidence_v0,
)
from bim_ai.kernel_ifc_opening_replay_v0 import build_wall_hosted_opening_replay_commands_v0
from bim_ai.material_assembly_resolve import (
    resolved_layers_for_floor,
    resolved_layers_for_roof,
    resolved_layers_for_wall,
)
from bim_ai.roof_geometry import (
    footprint_is_valid_axis_aligned_rectangle_mm,
    gable_half_run_mm_and_ridge_axis,
)

try:
    import ifcopenshell  # noqa: F401
    import ifcopenshell.util.element as ifc_elem_util
    import ifcopenshell.util.placement as ifc_placement

54	        "on" always adds 1 h, "off" never adds DST.

56	        as round(lon_deg / 15).

92	
93	    # Geometric Mean Anomaly of the Sun (degrees).
94	    M = 357.52911 + T * (35999.05029 - 0.0001537 * T)
95	
96	    # Earth Orbit Eccentricity.
97	    e = 0.016708634 - T * (0.000042037 + 0.0000001267 * T)

100	    M_rad = math.radians(M)

104	        + 0.000289 * math.sin(3 * M_rad)
105	    )

108	    sun_lon = L0 + C

116	        26 + (21.448 - T * (46.8150 + T * (0.00059 - T * 0.001813))) / 60
117	    ) / 60
118	
119	    # Corrected Obliquity.
120	    obliq_corr = mean_obliq + 0.00256 * math.cos(math.radians(omega))
121	
122	    # Sun's Declination.
123	    declination = math.degrees(

126	        )

131	    L0_rad = math.radians(L0)
132	    M_rad2 = math.radians(M)

138	        - 1.25 * e * e * math.sin(2 * M_rad2)
139	    )

"""SUN-V3-01: NOAA solar position algorithm — pure Python, no external dependencies.

Public API
----------
compute_sun_position(lat_deg, lon_deg, date_iso, hours, minutes,
                     dst_strategy="auto", utc_offset_hours=None)
    -> (azimuth_deg, elevation_deg)

Reference: NOAA Solar Calculator spreadsheet (deterministic).
Expected: Munich 48.13°N, 11.58°E, 21 June, 14:30 CEST → ~216° az, ~53° el.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import math


def _julian_day(year: int, month: int, day: int, ut_hours: float) -> float:
    """Compute Julian Day Number for a Gregorian calendar date + UT hours."""
    if month <= 2:
        year -= 1
        month += 12
    A = math.floor(year / 100)
    B = 2 - A + math.floor(A / 4)
    return (
        math.floor(365.25 * (year + 4716))
        + math.floor(30.6001 * (month + 1))
        + day
        + ut_hours / 24
        + B
        - 1524.5
    )


def compute_sun_position(
    lat_deg: float,
    lon_deg: float,
    date_iso: str,  # YYYY-MM-DD
    hours: int,
    minutes: int,
    dst_strategy: str = "auto",  # "auto" | "on" | "off"
    utc_offset_hours: float | None = None,  # if None, derived from longitude
) -> tuple[float, float]:  # (azimuth_deg, elevation_deg)
    """Compute solar azimuth and corrected elevation using the NOAA algorithm.

    Parameters
    ----------
    lat_deg: Latitude in decimal degrees (negative = south).
    lon_deg: Longitude in decimal degrees (negative = west).
    date_iso: Date string in YYYY-MM-DD format.
    hours: Local clock hour (0–23).
    minutes: Local clock minute (0–59).
    dst_strategy: "auto" applies a simplified hemisphere-based DST rule,
        "on" always adds 1 h, "off" never adds DST.
    utc_offset_hours: Standard UTC offset in hours. If None, approximated
        as round(lon_deg / 15).

    Returns
    -------
    (azimuth_deg, elevation_deg) — azimuth measured clockwise from north,
    elevation corrected for atmospheric refraction.
    """
    year, month, day = map(int, date_iso.split("-"))

    # Derive UTC offset from longitude if not provided.
    if utc_offset_hours is None:
        utc_offset_hours = round(lon_deg / 15.0)

    # Apply DST adjustment.
    dst_hours = 0
    if dst_strategy == "on":
        dst_hours = 1
    elif dst_strategy == "auto":
        # Northern hemisphere: DST roughly March–October (simplified).
        if lat_deg >= 0 and 3 <= month <= 10:
            dst_hours = 1
        # Southern hemisphere: DST roughly September–April (simplified).
        elif lat_deg < 0 and (month >= 9 or month <= 4):
            dst_hours = 1

    # Convert local time to UT, normalised to [0, 24).
    local_hours = hours + minutes / 60.0
    ut_hours = (local_hours - utc_offset_hours - dst_hours) % 24

    jd = _julian_day(year, month, day, ut_hours)

    # Julian Century from J2000.0.
    T = (jd - 2451545.0) / 36525.0

    # Geometric Mean Longitude of the Sun (degrees).
    L0 = (280.46646 + T * (36000.76983 + T * 0.0003032)) % 360

    # Geometric Mean Anomaly of the Sun (degrees).
    M = 357.52911 + T * (35999.05029 - 0.0001537 * T)

    # Earth Orbit Eccentricity.
    e = 0.016708634 - T * (0.000042037 + 0.0000001267 * T)

    # Sun's Equation of Center.
    M_rad = math.radians(M)
    C = (
        (1.914602 - T * (0.004817 + 0.000014 * T)) * math.sin(M_rad)
        + (0.019993 - 0.000101 * T) * math.sin(2 * M_rad)
        + 0.000289 * math.sin(3 * M_rad)
    )

    # Sun's True Longitude.
    sun_lon = L0 + C

    # Sun's Apparent Longitude (aberration + nutation).
    omega = 125.04 - 1934.136 * T
    apparent_lon = sun_lon - 0.00569 - 0.00478 * math.sin(math.radians(omega))

    # Mean Obliquity of Ecliptic.
    mean_obliq = 23 + (
        26 + (21.448 - T * (46.8150 + T * (0.00059 - T * 0.001813))) / 60
    ) / 60

    # Corrected Obliquity.
    obliq_corr = mean_obliq + 0.00256 * math.cos(math.radians(omega))

    # Sun's Declination.
    declination = math.degrees(
        math.asin(
            math.sin(math.radians(obliq_corr)) * math.sin(math.radians(apparent_lon))
        )
    )

    # Equation of Time (minutes).
    y = math.tan(math.radians(obliq_corr / 2)) ** 2
    L0_rad = math.radians(L0)
    M_rad2 = math.radians(M)
    eot = 4 * math.degrees(
        y * math.sin(2 * L0_rad)
        - 2 * e * math.sin(M_rad2)
        + 4 * e * y * math.sin(M_rad2) * math.cos(2 * L0_rad)
        - 0.5 * y * y * math.sin(4 * L0_rad)
        - 1.25 * e * e * math.sin(2 * M_rad2)
    )

    # True Solar Time (minutes).
    true_solar_time = (ut_hours * 60 + eot + 4 * lon_deg) % 1440

    # Hour Angle (degrees).
    if true_solar_time / 4 < 0:
        hour_angle = true_solar_time / 4 + 180
    else:
        hour_angle = true_solar_time / 4 - 180

    # Solar Zenith Angle.
    lat_rad = math.radians(lat_deg)
    decl_rad = math.radians(declination)
    ha_rad = math.radians(hour_angle)
    cos_zenith = math.sin(lat_rad) * math.sin(decl_rad) + math.cos(lat_rad) * math.cos(
        decl_rad
    ) * math.cos(ha_rad)
    cos_zenith = max(-1.0, min(1.0, cos_zenith))
    zenith = math.degrees(math.acos(cos_zenith))

    # Solar Elevation (before atmospheric refraction correction).
    elevation = 90 - zenith

    # Atmospheric refraction correction (approximate).
    if elevation > 85:
        refraction = 0.0
    elif elevation > 5:
        refraction = (
            58.1 / math.tan(math.radians(elevation))
            - 0.07 / math.tan(math.radians(elevation)) ** 3
            + 0.000086 / math.tan(math.radians(elevation)) ** 5
        ) / 3600
    elif elevation > -0.575:
        refraction = (
            1735
            + elevation
            * (-518.2 + elevation * (103.4 + elevation * (-12.79 + elevation * 0.711)))
        ) / 3600
    else:
        refraction = -20.772 / math.tan(math.radians(elevation)) / 3600

    elevation_corrected = elevation + refraction

    # Solar Azimuth (degrees, clockwise from north).
    cos_az_num = math.sin(lat_rad) * math.cos(math.radians(zenith)) - math.sin(decl_rad)
    cos_az_den = math.cos(lat_rad) * math.sin(math.radians(zenith))

    if abs(cos_az_den) < 1e-10:
        azimuth = 0.0
    else:
        cos_az = max(-1.0, min(1.0, cos_az_num / cos_az_den))
        azimuth_raw = math.degrees(math.acos(cos_az))
        if hour_angle > 0:
            azimuth = (azimuth_raw + 180) % 360
        else:
            azimuth = (540 - azimuth_raw) % 360

    return azimuth, elevation_corrected

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator, model_validator

from bim_ai.cmd.types import AgentTrace
from bim_ai.roof_geometry import RoofGeometryMode

# --- Geometry primitives ------------------------------------------------------------


class Vec2Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")


class Vec3Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")


class CameraMm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    position: Vec3Mm
    target: Vec3Mm
    up: Vec3Mm


EvidenceRefKind = Literal["sheet", "viewpoint", "plan_view", "section_cut", "deterministic_png"]


class EvidenceRef(BaseModel):
    """BCF/issue pointer into deterministic evidence rows or PNG basenames."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: EvidenceRefKind
    sheet_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sheetId")
    viewpoint_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="viewpointId")
    plan_view_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="planViewId")
    section_cut_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sectionCutId")
    png_basename: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="pngBasename")


WallLayerFunction = Literal["structure", "insulation", "finish"]
WallBasisLine = Literal["center", "face_interior", "face_exterior"]
PlanDetailLevelPlan = Literal["coarse", "medium", "fine"]
PhaseFilter = Literal["all", "existing", "demolition", "new"]

_SCHEME_HEX_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$")


class ProjectSettingsElem(BaseModel):
    """Singleton-style project datum (canonical units / locale metadata)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["project_settings"] = "project_settings"
    id: str
    length_unit: str = Field(default="millimeter", alias="lengthUnit")
    angular_unit_deg: str = Field(default="degree", alias="angularUnitDeg")

    display_locale: str = Field(default="en-US", alias="displayLocale")


class RoomColorSchemeRow(BaseModel):
    """One programme and/or department → fill colour for room-scheme presentation."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    programme_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="programmeCode")
    department: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="department")
    scheme_color_hex: str = Field(alias="schemeColorHex")

    @field_validator("programme_code", "department", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def _strip_optional_str(cls, v: Any) -> str | None:
        if v is None:

model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["beam"] = "beam"
    id: str
    name: str = "Beam"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=200, gt=0)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=400, gt=0)
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    start_column_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="startColumnId")
    end_column_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="endColumnId")
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")


class CeilingElem(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — flat ceiling slab bounded by a closed polygon at a level.

    ``heightOffsetMm`` is measured from the host level elevation; positive
    values raise the ceiling above the level. Distinct from ``floor``
    because ceilings hang from above and host downward-facing finishes.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["ceiling"] = "ceiling"
    id: str
    name: str = "Ceiling"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    height_offset_mm: float = Field(default=2700, alias="heightOffsetMm")
    thickness_mm: float = Field(default=20, alias="thicknessMm", gt=0)
    ceiling_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ceilingTypeId")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")


ConstraintRule = Literal[
    "equal_distance",
    "equal_length",
    "parallel",
    "perpendicular",
    "collinear",
]
ConstraintAnchor = Literal["start", "end", "mid", "center"]


class ConstraintRefRow(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
    anchor: ConstraintAnchor = "center"


class ConstraintElem(BaseModel):
    """EDT-02 — geometric constraint between element groups.

    Engine evaluates constraints after each command apply and rejects
    commands that would violate any `error`-severity constraint. Locked
    distances (`equal_distance` with `lockedValueMm`) are the most common
    case, set via the padlock UI on a temporary dimension.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["constraint"] = "constraint"
    id: str
    name: str = ""
    rule: ConstraintRule
    refs_a: list[ConstraintRefRow] = Field(alias="refsA")
    refs_b: list[ConstraintRefRow] = Field(alias="refsB")
    locked_value_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="lockedValueMm")
    severity: Literal["warning", "error"] = "error"
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)


class MassElem(BaseModel):
    """SKB-02 — volumetric massing primitive.

    An axis-aligned (or rotated) box representing a building mass before
    walls are authored. Used during the SKB-12 cookbook's massing phase
    so the agent can iterate on volumes before committing to walls.

    A subsequent `materializeMassToWalls` engine command (deferred)
    auto-extracts walls + floor + roof-stub from each mass so the agent
    never starts the wall phase from a blank canvas.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["mass"] = "mass"
    id: str
    name: str = "Mass"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    footprint_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(
        alias="footprintMm",
        description=(
            "Closed polygon of the mass's plan footprint (≥3 vertices). "
            "Axis-aligned rectangles use 4 corners; arbitrary polygons OK."
        ),
    )
    height_mm: float = Field(default=3000, alias="heightMm", gt=0)
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0, alias="rotationDeg")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    phase_id: str | None = Field(
        default="massing",
        alias="phaseId",
        description="SKB-08 phase tag; defaults to 'massing'.",
    )
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")


class VoidCutElem(BaseModel):
    """SKT-01 — subtractive-boolean marker against a host element.

    The geometry is a closed profile + extrusion depth; the renderer is
    responsible for performing the actual CSG. The element exists in the
    document so it survives undo / redo and IFC export.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["void_cut"] = "void_cut"
    id: str
    host_element_id: str = Field(alias="hostElementId")
    profile_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="profileMm")
    depth_mm: float = Field(alias="depthMm", gt=0)


class PhaseElem(BaseModel):
    """KRN-V3-01 — project-level phasing primitive.

    Default chain: Existing (ord=0) → Demolition (ord=1) → New (ord=2).
    ``ord`` governs display order and element classification: phase_created.ord ==
    view.phase.ord → new; < view.phase.ord → existing or demolition.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["phase"] = "phase"
    id: str
    name: str
    ord: int = 0


class TokenSlot(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    name: str
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    font_size_mm: float = Field(default=3.5, alias="fontSizeMm")


class TitleblockTypeElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["titleblock_type"] = "titleblock_type"
    id: str
    name: str
    svg_template: str = Field(default="", alias="svgTemplate")
    token_slots: list[TokenSlot] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="tokenSlots")

case CreateBalconyCmd():
            bid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if bid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{bid}'")
            if cmd.wall_id not in els or not isinstance(els[cmd.wall_id], WallElem):
                raise ValueError("createBalcony.wallId must reference an existing wall")
            els[bid] = BalconyElem(
                kind="balcony",
                id=bid,
                name=cmd.name,
                wall_id=cmd.wall_id,
                elevation_mm=cmd.elevation_mm,
                projection_mm=cmd.projection_mm,
                slab_thickness_mm=cmd.slab_thickness_mm,
                balustrade_height_mm=cmd.balustrade_height_mm,
            )

        case CreateSweepCmd():
            sid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if sid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{sid}'")
            if cmd.level_id not in els or not isinstance(els[cmd.level_id], LevelElem):
                raise ValueError("createSweep.levelId must reference an existing Level")
            if len(cmd.path_mm) < 2:
                raise ValueError("createSweep.pathMm requires ≥2 points")
            if len(cmd.profile_mm) < 3:
                raise ValueError("createSweep.profileMm requires ≥3 points (closed loop)")
            if cmd.profile_plane not in ("normal_to_path_start", "work_plane"):
                raise ValueError(
                    "createSweep.profilePlane must be 'normal_to_path_start' or 'work_plane'"
                )
            if cmd.material_key is not None and resolve_material(cmd.material_key) is None:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"createSweep.materialKey '{cmd.material_key}' is not in the material catalog"
                )
            els[sid] = SweepElem(
                kind="sweep",
                id=sid,
                name=cmd.name,
                level_id=cmd.level_id,
                path_mm=list(cmd.path_mm),
                profile_mm=list(cmd.profile_mm),
                profile_plane=cmd.profile_plane,
                material_key=cmd.material_key,
            )

        case CreateDormerCmd():
            did = cmd.id or new_id()
            if did in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{did}'")

# Other element kinds: silently ignored.
    return None


def mirror_advisories_for_command(doc: Document, cmd: MirrorElementsCmd) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Compute non-blocking advisories for a mirror command.

    Returned shape: ``{"code": "mirror_asymmetric", "elementId": <id>}`` for
    each door/window whose family type is in
    ``cmd.asymmetric_family_type_ids``. The opening is still mirrored
    (degraded but useful) — callers surface the advisory in the UI.
    """
    asymmetric: set[str] = set(cmd.asymmetric_family_type_ids)
    if not asymmetric:
        return []
    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for tid in cmd.element_ids:
        el = doc.elements.get(tid)
        if isinstance(el, (DoorElem, WindowElem)) and el.family_type_id in asymmetric:
            out.append({"code": "mirror_asymmetric", "elementId": tid})
    return out


def diff_undo_cmds(prev_doc: Document, next_doc: Document) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    cmds: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    prev_ids = set(prev_doc.elements.keys())
    next_ids = set(next_doc.elements.keys())

    delete_ids = sorted(next_ids - prev_ids)
    for nid in delete_ids:
        cmds.append({"type": "deleteElement", "elementId": nid})

    for pid in sorted(prev_ids.union(next_ids)):
        pv = prev_doc.elements.get(pid)
        nx = next_doc.elements.get(pid)
        if nx != pv and pv is not None:
            cmds.append({"type": "restoreElement", "element": pv.model_dump(by_alias=True)})
    return cmds


def compute_delta_wire(prev_doc: Document, next_doc: Document) -> dict[str, Any]:
    removed_ids = sorted(prev_doc.elements.keys() - next_doc.elements.keys())
    elements_patch: dict[str, Any] = {}
    next_ids_all = next_doc.elements.keys()
    prev_ids_all = prev_doc.elements.keys()
    union = sorted(set(next_ids_all) | set(prev_ids_all))
    for eid in union:
        p = prev_doc.elements.get(eid)
        n = next_doc.elements.get(eid)

class FloorElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["floor"] = "floor"
    id: str
    name: str = "Floor"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", default=220)
    structure_thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="structureThicknessMm", default=140)
    finish_thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="finishThicknessMm", default=0)
    floor_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="floorTypeId")
    insulation_extension_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="insulationExtensionMm")
    room_bounded: bool = Field(default=False, alias="roomBounded")
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_id: str | None = Field(
        default=None,
        alias="phaseId",
        description="SKB-08 phase tag carried forward when materialised from a mass.",
    )
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
    agent_trace: AgentTrace | None = Field(default=None, alias="agentTrace")
    option_set_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="optionSetId")
    option_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="optionId")


class RoofElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)

class ToposolidElem(BaseModel):
    """TOP-V3-01 — terrain solid primitive.

    Closed XY boundary polygon + height samples (sparse surveyor data) or
    regular grid (DEM raster), extruded downward by ``thicknessMm``.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["toposolid"] = "toposolid"
    id: str
    name: str | None = None
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    height_samples: list[HeightSample] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="heightSamples")
    heightmap_grid_mm: HeightmapGrid | None = Field(default=None, alias="heightmapGridMm")
    thickness_mm: float = Field(default=1500.0, alias="thicknessMm")
    base_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseElevationMm")
    default_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultMaterialKey")
    pinned: bool = False

file.content:
class CreateLinkModelCmd(BaseModel):
    """FED-01: insert a ``link_model`` element pointing at another bim-ai model.

    Engine-level validation rejects empty ``sourceModelId`` and self-reference
    (``sourceModelId`` matching this link's own id). Existence in DB and
    circular-link BFS are validated by the route handler that has DB access.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createLinkModel"] = "createLinkModel"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Linked model"
    source_model_id: str = Field(alias="sourceModelId")
    source_model_revision: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="sourceModelRevision")
    position_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    origin_alignment_mode: Literal[
        "origin_to_origin", "project_origin", "shared_coords"

content.text: Now I'll add the engine handlers. First, let me understand the `Poly` class to use for the pierce check:

content.content:
17	class Poly:
18	    __slots__ = ("verts",)
19	
20	    def __init__(self, verts: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]) -> None:
21	        self.verts = verts
22	
23	
24	def wall_corners(
25	    start_mm: tuple[float, float],
26	    end_mm: tuple[float, float],
27	    thickness_mm: float,
28	) -> Poly:
29	    sx, sy = start_mm
30	    ex, ey = end_mm
31	    dx = ex - sx
32	    dy = ey - sy
33	    length_sq = dx * dx + dy * dy
34	    if length_sq < 1e-6:
35	        return Poly(((sx, sy),))
36	    length = length_sq**0.5
37	    ux, uy = dx / length, dy / length
38	    px, py = -uy, ux
39	    half_l = length / 2
40	    half_t = thickness_mm / 2
41	    cx = (sx + ex) / 2
42	    cy = (sy + ey) / 2
43	
44	    def wc(salong: float, sacross: float) -> tuple[float, float]:
45	        return cx + ux * salong + px * sacross, cy + uy * salong + py * sacross
46	
47	    pts = (
48	        wc(+half_l, +half_t),
49	        wc(+half_l, -half_t),
50	        wc(-half_l, -half_t),
51	        wc(-half_l, +half_t),
52	    )
53	    return Poly(pts)
54

file.content:
def _dormer_overflow_point_in_polygon(
    px: float, py: float, poly: list[tuple[float, float]]
) -> bool:
    n = len(poly)
    if n < 3:
        return False
    inside = False
    j = n - 1
    for i in range(n):
        xi, yi = poly[i]
        xj, yj = poly[j]
        if ((yi > py) != (yj > py)) and (
            px < (xj - xi) * (py - yi) / (yj - yi + 1e-12) + xi
        ):
            inside = not inside
        j = i
    return inside


def _room_boundary_open_violations(elements: dict[str, Element]) -> list[Violation]:
    doc_snap = Document(revision=0, elements=dict(elements))
    unbounded_ids = detect_unbounded_rooms_v1(doc_snap)
    out: list[Violation] = []
    for rid in unbounded_ids:
        el = elements.get(rid)
        name = el.name if hasattr(el, "name") else rid
        out.append(
            Violation(
                rule_id="room_boundary_open",
                severity="warning",
                message=(
                    f"Room '{name}' ({rid}) has an open boundary — "
                    "it is not fully enclosed by axis-aligned walls or room separations."
                ),
                element_ids=[rid],
            )
        )
    return out


def _monitored_source_drift_advisory_violations(
    elements: dict[str, Element],
) -> list[Violation]:
    """FED-03: surface elements whose ``monitor_source.drifted`` flag is set.

    The drift state is computed lazily by the ``BumpMonitoredRevisions``
    command (which has access to a source provider); this rule just reads
    what the bump command wrote so it can run inside the synchronous
    constraint evaluator.
    """

input.content:
"""Toposolid geometry helpers — Delaunay triangulation + contour extraction."""
from __future__ import annotations

from bim_ai.elements import HeightSample, ToposolidElem


def samples_from_toposolid(topo: ToposolidElem) -> list[tuple[float, float, float]]:
    """Return (xMm, yMm, zMm) tuples from whichever parametrisation is active."""
    if topo.heightmap_grid_mm:
        g = topo.heightmap_grid_mm
        pts = []
        for r in range(g.rows):
            for c in range(g.cols):
                x = c * g.step_mm
                y = r * g.step_mm
                z = g.values[r * g.cols + c]
                pts.append((x, y, z))
        return pts
    return [(s.x_mm, s.y_mm, s.z_mm) for s in topo.height_samples]


def contour_polylines(
    topo: ToposolidElem, interval_mm: float = 500.0
) -> list[list[tuple[float, float]]]:
    """Return XY polylines representing contour lines at ``interval_mm`` spacing.

    Uses linear interpolation between triangulated edges. Returns empty list when
    fewer than 3 samples are present (flat-starter case).
    """
    pts = samples_from_toposolid(topo)
    if len(pts) < 3:
        return []
    z_vals = [p[2] for p in pts]
    z_min = min(z_vals)
    z_max = max(z_vals)
    levels: list[float] = []
    z = z_min + interval_mm
    while z < z_max:
        levels.append(z)
        z += interval_mm
    # Stub: renderer fills in real geometry via Delaunay triangulation.
    return [[] for _ in levels]


def underside_elevation_mm(topo: ToposolidElem) -> float:
    """Elevation of the flat underside of the toposolid solid."""
    base = topo.base_elevation_mm or 0.0
    return base - topo.thickness_mm

def samples_from_toposolid(topo: ToposolidElem) -> list[tuple[float, float, float]]:
    """Return (xMm, yMm, zMm) tuples from whichever parametrisation is active."""
    if topo.heightmap_grid_mm:

                x = c * g.step_mm

        return pts

def contour_polylines(
    topo: ToposolidElem, interval_mm: float = 500.0
) -> list[list[tuple[float, float]]]:
    """Return XY polylines representing contour lines at ``interval_mm`` spacing.

    Uses linear interpolation between triangulated edges. Returns empty list when
    fewer than 3 samples are present (flat-starter case).
    """
    pts = samples_from_toposolid(topo)
    if len(pts) < 3:
        return []
    z_vals = [p[2] for p in pts]
    z_min = min(z_vals)
    z_max = max(z_vals)

    z = z_min + interval_mm
    while z < z_max:

        z += interval_mm
    # Stub: renderer fills in real geometry via Delaunay triangulation.
    return [[] for _ in levels]


def underside_elevation_mm(topo: ToposolidElem) -> float:

    base = topo.base_elevation_mm or 0.0
    return base - topo.thickness_mm

400	def _kernel_ifc_space_export_props(rm: RoomElem) -> dict[str, str]:
401	    """Optional Pset_SpaceCommon fields for IDS / room programme read-back (kernel slice)."""
402	
403	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
404	    if rm.programme_code:
405	        out["ProgrammeCode"] = rm.programme_code

413	
414	
415	def _ifc_product_defines_qto_template(product: Any, qto_template_name: str) -> bool:
416	    rels = getattr(product, "IsDefinedBy", None) or []
417	    for rel in rels:
418	        try:
419	            if not rel.is_a("IfcRelDefinesByProperties"):
420	                continue
421	        except Exception:
422	            continue
423	        dfn = getattr(rel, "RelatingPropertyDefinition", None)
424	        if dfn is None:
425	            continue
426	        try:
427	            if dfn.is_a("IfcElementQuantity") and getattr(dfn, "Name", None) == qto_template_name:
428	                return True
429	        except Exception:
430	            continue
431	    return False
432	
433	
434	def _count_ifc_products_with_qto_template(products: list[Any], qto_template_name: str) -> int:
435	    """Count IFC products that define an ``IfcElementQuantity`` with ``Name == qto_template_name``."""
436	
437	    return sum(1 for p in products if _ifc_product_defines_qto_template(p, qto_template_name))
438	
439	
440	def _read_named_qto_values(product: Any, qto_template_name: str) -> dict[str, float]:
441	    """Read scalar quantity values from a named IfcElementQuantity attached to a product."""
442	    rels = getattr(product, "IsDefinedBy", None) or []
443	    for rel in rels:
444	        try:
445	            if not rel.is_a("IfcRelDefinesByProperties"):
446	                continue
447	        except Exception:
448	            continue
449	        dfn = getattr(rel, "RelatingPropertyDefinition", None)
450	        if dfn is None:
451	            continue
452	        try:
453	            if not dfn.is_a("IfcElementQuantity"):
454	                continue
455	            if getattr(dfn, "Name", None) != qto_template_name:
456	                continue
457	        except Exception:
458	            continue
459	        result: dict[str, float] = {}
460	        for qty in getattr(dfn, "Quantities", None) or []:
461	            name = str(getattr(qty, "Name", None) or "")
462	            for attr in ("CountValue", "LengthValue", "AreaValue", "VolumeValue", "WeightValue"):
463	                val = getattr(qty, attr, None)
464	                if val is not None:
465	                    try:
466	                        result[name] = float(val)
467	                    except (ValueError, TypeError):
468	                        pass
469	                    break
470	        return result
471	    return {}

input.content:
"""TOP-V3-01 — Toposolid primitive tests."""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import uuid

import pytest

from bim_ai.commands import (
    CreateToposolidCmd,
    DeleteToposolidCmd,
    UpdateToposolidCmd,
)
from bim_ai.constraints import evaluate
from bim_ai.document import Document
from bim_ai.elements import (
    FloorElem,
    HeightSample,
    HeightmapGrid,
    SlabOpeningElem,
    ToposolidElem,
    Vec2Mm,
)
from bim_ai.engine import apply_commands
from bim_ai.site.toposolid import (
    contour_polylines,
    samples_from_toposolid,
    underside_elevation_mm,
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _doc_with(*elements):
    els = {e.id: e for e in elements}
    return Document(revision=1, elements=els)


def _boundary():
    return [
        {"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0},
        {"xMm": 10000, "yMm": 0},
        {"xMm": 10000, "yMm": 10000},
        {"xMm": 0, "yMm": 10000},
    ]


def _height_samples():
    return [
        {"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0, "zMm": 0},
        {"xMm": 5000, "yMm": 0, "zMm": 500},
        {"xMm": 10000, "yMm": 0, "zMm": 1000},
        {"xMm": 5000, "yMm": 5000, "zMm": 750},
    ]


def _heightmap_grid():
    return {
        "stepMm": 5000,
        "rows": 2,
        "cols": 2,
        "values": [0.0, 500.0, 250.0, 750.0],
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Engine: CreateToposolidCmd
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_create_toposolid_sparse_samples():
    doc = _doc_with()
    cmd = CreateToposolidCmd(
        toposolidId="topo-1",
        boundaryMm=_boundary(),
        heightSamples=_height_samples(),
    )
    new_doc = apply_commands(doc, [cmd.model_dump(by_alias=True)])
    assert "topo-1" in new_doc.elements
    el = new_doc.elements["topo-1"]
    assert isinstance(el, ToposolidElem)
    assert el.kind == "toposolid"
    assert len(el.height_samples) == 4
    assert el.thickness_mm == 1500.0


def test_create_toposolid_heightmap_grid():
    doc = _doc_with()
    cmd = CreateToposolidCmd(
        toposolidId="topo-grid",
        boundaryMm=_boundary(),
        heightmapGridMm=_heightmap_grid(),
    )
    new_doc = apply_commands(doc, [cmd.model_dump(by_alias=True)])
    el = new_doc.elements["topo-grid"]
    assert isinstance(el, ToposolidElem)
    assert el.heightmap_grid_mm is not None
    assert el.heightmap_grid_mm.rows == 2
    assert el.heightmap_grid_mm.cols == 2


def test_create_toposolid_flat_starter():
    """Empty height_samples (no grid) is accepted as a flat-starter terrain."""
    doc = _doc_with()
    cmd = CreateToposolidCmd(
        toposolidId="topo-flat",
        boundaryMm=_boundary(),
    )
    new_doc = apply_commands(doc, [cmd.model_dump(by_alias=True)])
    el = new_doc.elements["topo-flat"]
    assert isinstance(el, ToposolidElem)
    assert el.height_samples == []
    assert el.heightmap_grid_mm is None


def test_create_toposolid_duplicate_id_raises():
    doc = _doc_with()
    cmd = CreateToposolidCmd(toposolidId="topo-1", boundaryMm=_boundary())
    new_doc = apply_commands(doc, [cmd.model_dump(by_alias=True)])
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="already exists"):
        apply_commands(new_doc, [cmd.model_dump(by_alias=True)])


def test_create_toposolid_too_few_boundary_points_raises():
    doc = _doc_with()
    cmd = CreateToposolidCmd(
        toposolidId="topo-bad",
        boundaryMm=[{"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0}, {"xMm": 1000, "yMm": 0}],
    )
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="at least 3"):
        apply_commands(doc, [cmd.model_dump(by_alias=True)])


def test_create_toposolid_both_parametrisations_raises():
    doc = _doc_with()
    cmd = CreateToposolidCmd(
        toposolidId="topo-bad",
        boundaryMm=_boundary(),
        heightSamples=_height_samples(),
        heightmapGridMm=_heightmap_grid(),
    )
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="not both"):
        apply_commands(doc, [cmd.model_dump(by_alias=True)])


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Engine: UpdateToposolidCmd
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_update_toposolid_patches_thickness():
    doc = _doc_with()
    create = CreateToposolidCmd(
        toposolidId="topo-1",
        boundaryMm=_boundary(),
        heightSamples=_height_samples(),
        thicknessMm=1500,
    )
    doc = apply_commands(doc, [create.model_dump(by_alias=True)])

    update = UpdateToposolidCmd(toposolidId="topo-1", thicknessMm=2000)
    doc = apply_commands(doc, [update.model_dump(by_alias=True)])

    el = doc.elements["topo-1"]
    assert isinstance(el, ToposolidElem)
    assert el.thickness_mm == 2000
    assert len(el.height_samples) == 4  # unchanged


def test_update_toposolid_missing_id_raises():
    doc = _doc_with()
    update = UpdateToposolidCmd(toposolidId="nonexistent", thicknessMm=2000)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="no toposolid"):
        apply_commands(doc, [update.model_dump(by_alias=True)])


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Engine: DeleteToposolidCmd
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_delete_toposolid_removes_element():
    doc = _doc_with()
    create = CreateToposolidCmd(toposolidId="topo-1", boundaryMm=_boundary())
    doc = apply_commands(doc, [create.model_dump(by_alias=True)])
    assert "topo-1" in doc.elements

    delete = DeleteToposolidCmd(toposolidId="topo-1")
    doc = apply_commands(doc, [delete.model_dump(by_alias=True)])
    assert "topo-1" not in doc.elements


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# site/toposolid helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_samples_from_toposolid_sparse():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="t1",
        boundaryMm=[Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000)],
        heightSamples=[
            HeightSample(xMm=0, yMm=0, zMm=100),
            HeightSample(xMm=5000, yMm=0, zMm=200),
        ],
    )
    pts = samples_from_toposolid(topo)
    assert pts == [(0.0, 0.0, 100.0), (5000.0, 0.0, 200.0)]


def test_samples_from_toposolid_grid():
    grid = HeightmapGrid(stepMm=5000, rows=2, cols=2, values=[0.0, 100.0, 200.0, 300.0])
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="t2",
        boundaryMm=[Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000)],
        heightmapGridMm=grid,
    )
    pts = samples_from_toposolid(topo)
    assert len(pts) == 4
    assert pts[0] == (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
    assert pts[1] == (5000.0, 0.0, 100.0)
    assert pts[2] == (0.0, 5000.0, 200.0)
    assert pts[3] == (5000.0, 5000.0, 300.0)


def test_contour_polylines_flat_starter_returns_empty():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="t-flat",
        boundaryMm=[Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000)],
    )
    assert contour_polylines(topo) == []


def test_contour_polylines_two_samples_returns_empty():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="t-few",
        boundaryMm=[Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000)],
        heightSamples=[
            HeightSample(xMm=0, yMm=0, zMm=0),
            HeightSample(xMm=5000, yMm=0, zMm=1000),
        ],
    )
    assert contour_polylines(topo) == []


def test_underside_elevation_mm_default_base():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="t1",
        boundaryMm=[Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=1000)],
        thicknessMm=1500,
    )
    assert underside_elevation_mm(topo) == -1500.0


def test_underside_elevation_mm_with_base():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="t1",
        boundaryMm=[Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=0), Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=1000)],
        thicknessMm=1500,
        baseElevationMm=500,
    )
    assert underside_elevation_mm(topo) == -1000.0


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Advisory: toposolid_pierce_check
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_toposolid_pierce_check_fires_on_overlap():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="topo-1",
        boundaryMm=[
            Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=20000, yMm=0),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=20000, yMm=20000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=20000),
        ],
    )
    floor = FloorElem(
        id="floor-1",
        name="Ground Floor",
        levelId="lv1",
        boundaryMm=[
            Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=1000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=1000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=5000),
        ],
        thicknessMm=200,
    )
    doc = _doc_with(topo, floor)
    violations = evaluate(doc.elements)
    pierce_viols = [v for v in violations if v.rule_id == "toposolid_pierce_check"]
    assert len(pierce_viols) >= 1
    assert pierce_viols[0].severity == "warning"
    assert not pierce_viols[0].blocking


def test_toposolid_pierce_check_suppressed_by_slab_opening():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="topo-1",
        boundaryMm=[
            Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=20000, yMm=0),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=20000, yMm=20000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=20000),
        ],
    )
    floor = FloorElem(
        id="floor-1",
        name="Ground Floor",
        levelId="lv1",
        boundaryMm=[
            Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=1000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=1000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=5000),
        ],
        thicknessMm=200,
    )
    opening = SlabOpeningElem(
        id="opening-1",
        name="Basement cut",
        hostFloorId="floor-1",
        boundaryMm=[
            Vec2Mm(xMm=1000, yMm=1000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=1000),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000),
        ],
    )
    doc = _doc_with(topo, floor, opening)
    violations = evaluate(doc.elements)
    pierce_viols = [v for v in violations if v.rule_id == "toposolid_pierce_check"]
    assert len(pierce_viols) == 0


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Round-trip: Document with Toposolid
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_document_roundtrip_with_toposolid():
    topo = ToposolidElem(
        id="topo-rt",
        boundaryMm=[
            Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=0),
            Vec2Mm(xMm=5000, yMm=5000),
        ],
        heightSamples=[
            HeightSample(xMm=0, yMm=0, zMm=0),
            HeightSample(xMm=5000, yMm=0, zMm=1000),
            HeightSample(xMm=5000, yMm=5000, zMm=500),
        ],
        thicknessMm=2000,
        baseElevationMm=100,
    )
    doc = _doc_with(topo)
    serialised = doc.model_dump(by_alias=True)
    restored = Document.model_validate(serialised)
    el = restored.elements["topo-rt"]
    assert isinstance(el, ToposolidElem)
    assert el.thickness_mm == 2000
    assert el.base_elevation_mm == 100
    assert len(el.height_samples) == 3
    assert el.height_samples[2].z_mm == 500
